assertions.text: A car pulled out in front of the narrator on the fast road (another vehicle moved into the narrator's path from ahead).
The narrator was traveling at around 110 km/h before braking.
The narrator performed an emergency brake (rapid deceleration) starting from around 110 km/h.
There was a single impact (the other car clipped the narrator's car once).
The narrator's car was clipped by the other car (the point of contact was caused by the other car striking the narrator's car).
The emergency braking occurred before the impact.
After the impact, the narrator slowed to walking pace.
The collision was not multiple; it was a single clip described as a 'clip' (implying a relatively minor impact rather than a major crash).

verdicts.evidence: The assertion about another vehicle pulling out into the narrator's path is not directly observable from the vehicle sensors alone; there is no camera or relative-vehicle track in the telemetry to show another vehicle's motion, so this is unverifiable.
GPS shows the vehicle speed at the start of the window was [113.0 km/h](T3) which is within ~15% of the claimed [110 km/h](T1) in the narrative.

Sensors show a sustained deceleration on accel_x with a peak signed deviation of [−0.777 g at t=1.12 s](T3) and the detect_events summary reports a sustained decel from [0.22 s to 4.11 s with mean magnitude 0.55 g](T2); GPS speed also dropped from [113.0 km/h to 78.0 km/h between t=0.0 and t=3.0 s](T2), consistent with emergency braking starting from around [113.0 km/h](T3).

The telemetry detects sustained deceleration and speed drops but does not include a distinct high‑magnitude impact spike (no impact-level accel >2.5 g reported), so the claim of exactly a single external contact event (one clip) is not determinable from sensors alone.

The sensors record accelerations but device-frame axes are not vehicle-aligned per the metadata, so whether the contact was caused by the other car striking this vehicle (vs. this vehicle striking something) cannot be determined from IMU/gps data alone.

Detect_events shows the sustained deceleration began at [0.22 s](T2) and there is no separate CISS impact spike; GPS speed drop from [113.0 km/h to 78.0 km/h between t=0.0 and t=3.0 s](T2) occurs concurrent with the deceleration, indicating braking started before or during any minor contact — consistent with braking occurring before impact.

GPS speed falls to low speeds later in the window, with [gps_speed last_value 42.0 km/h at t=15.0 s](T3) and earlier drops to [10.0 km/h at around t=8.0 s detected](T2) (speed drops detected from [26.0 to 10.0 km/h between t=6.0 and t=8.0 s](T2)), consistent with slowing to walking pace after the impact.

Telemetry shows no high-magnitude impact pulse above the impact threshold and only sustained deceleration; sensors alone cannot determine whether the contact was 'minor' as described, so the characterization as a relatively minor single clip is unverifiable from the available channels.

result.rationale: IMU and GPS strongly support high initial speed and an emergency braking event with large speed drops ([113.0 km/h start](T3); sustained decel [0.22–4.11 s, 0.55 g mean](T2); accel peak [−0.777 g at 1.12 s](T3)). However, there is no clear high‑g impact pulse to confirm a distinct single clip or establish which vehicle struck which, so assertions about other vehicle motion, single-impact count, impact direction, and minor severity are not verifiable from the telemetry alone.

result.notes: 100 Hz IMU over a 16 s window. Units: accel g, gyro deg/s, speed km/h. Device-to-vehicle axis alignment is NOT documented; do not assume which axis is longitudinal without cross-checking against GPS speed.

detections.note: direction ambiguous: device axis orientation undocumented

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|

accel_x.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_y.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned
